svg.text: Query Preprocessing — making the prompt better
Three techniques that fix the prompt BEFORE it hits the retriever
1. The big idea — user prompts are messy

text.tspan: Most retrieval failures aren't the retriever's fault.

svg.text: Real user prompts are wordy, vague, full of irrelevant context, or use different vocabulary than the documents they're trying to find.
Query preprocessing means transforming the raw prompt into something the retriever can match more reliably.

text.tspan: match the user's PROMPT against documents (a question vs answers — different shapes).
have an LLM write a fake "perfect answer" first, then match DOCUMENT vs DOCUMENT.

svg.text: space. HyDE pulls the query into
3. Named Entity Recognition (NER) — turn words into structured filters
Find specific kinds of things in the prompt (people, places, dates, books, organizations...) and use them as strict filters.

g.text: "I read The Great Gatsby by F. Scott Fitzgerald last summer while
visiting New York. Are there any similar books set in the 1920s

svg.text: NER engine (e.g. GLiNER, spaCy, or LLM-based extractor)
4. Query Rewriting — let an LLM clean the prompt first

g.text: "I was out walking my dog, a beautiful black lab named
Poppy, when she raced away from me and yanked on her
leash. Three days later my shoulder is still numb and my
fingers are all pins and needles. What's going on?"
irrelevant context: dog's name, color, the walk itself...
on the shoulder, resulting in persistent
shoulder and finger numbness for 3 days.
What are the potential causes such as
by a user to query a database of
to diagnoses. Rewrite it to optimize
it for searching the database by:
one extra LLM call → far fewer

svg.text: 5. Which to use when — a quick decision table

g.text: Skip when: queries and docs already
Use when: docs have metadata fields
Skip when: docs are unstructured prose
Almost always worth it: small cost,

svg.text: What's happened to these three techniques
● HyDE • less critical now — modern embedders (Voyage-3, text-embedding-3-large, Cohere v3+) handle question/answer asymmetry better.
● NER • the standalone library approach (GLiNER, spaCy) is often replaced by a single Haiku/GPT-4o-mini call returning structured JSON.
● Query rewriting • now baked into agentic search (Claude tool-use, Deep Research, Perplexity). Multi-query expansion is standard.
● Step-back prompting • generate an abstract version of the query for broader recall (Google Research)
● Query decomposition • break complex multi-part queries into sub-queries, retrieve each, merge results (LangChain, LlamaIndex)
